name: inline-loading
description: Compact inline status indicator that pairs a label with a loading, success, or error state

meta.name: InlineLoading
meta.description: Compact inline status indicator that pairs a label with a loading, success, or error state

props.name: labels
props.description: Overrides for the component’s user-visible strings (i18n).
props.type: { active?: string; finished?: string; error?: string }

whenToUse: Showing the progress of a single inline action — a Save button that becomes "Saving… / Saved / Failed"
Communicating async status next to a control without occupying its own block or modal
Reflecting a terminal success or error state that should be announced to assistive tech
whenNotToUse: Page- or section-level loading where layout space is reserved — use Skeleton or Spinner
Persistent, multi-line messages with recovery actions — use Alert or Notification
Determinate progress with a known percentage — use a progress bar

antiPatterns.bad: <Spinner /> with separate manually-managed success and error markup
antiPatterns.good: <InlineLoading status={status} /> driving icon and color from one status prop
antiPatterns.why: A single status keeps the icon, color, and announced label in sync and prevents mismatched states

related.name: Spinner
related.reason: InlineLoading renders a Spinner for its active state and adds finished/error icons plus a label

related.name: Alert
related.reason: Use Alert for persistent messages that need a title, body, and actions rather than a terse status

intent.a11yRationale: role="status" with aria-live="polite" announces the label when the status changes; the icon is decorative (aria-hidden) so meaning is carried by the text and color is never the sole signal

content.notes: Keep labels to a few words; the inactive status renders no label by default

flexibility.note: Defaults come from the i18n catalog per status; override with label or the per-status labels map
active must show a spinner, finished a success icon, error an error icon — do not repurpose
